condition.selected: The answer could be even or odd

incorrects.description: Student selected B: The answer could be even or odd

steps.text: Look at the second factor: 2. When you multiply any number by 2, you're counting by 2s. What kind of numbers do you get when you count by 2s?

incorrects.description: Student selected C: The answer is definitely odd

steps.text: Think about what times 2 means. You're multiplying by 2, which means counting by 2s. When you count by 2s, do you ever land on odd numbers?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: The factor 2 pulses, then a visual shows counting by 2s: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14.

steps.text: Let me show you the pattern. When you multiply by 2, you're skip-counting by 2s. Look: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14. Every number in that count is even. So 7 times 2 has to be even. The answer is definitely even. That's what the pattern tells you before you even compute.
